analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-443

POL-443.title: IMPLEMENT: SmartDocs 2.0 — OKF-conformant bundle foundations
POL-443.body:
## Objective

Give SmartDocs the OKF v0.1-conformant bundle foundations identified in the target architecture, while leaving all existing Polaris governance behavior unchanged.

## Context

[POL-441](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-441/analyze-redesigning-smartdocs-as-a-governed-okf-native-knowledge-layer) (analyze, this cluster's parent) asked whether SmartDocs should evolve into a governed OKF-native knowledge layer. The resulting design spec is `smartdocs/raw/smartdocs-2-0-architecture.md` (revision 2) — compares current SmartDocs to OKF v0.1, concludes SmartDocs should adopt OKF as a compatibility floor (§7.1) without changing its governance layer. This cluster implements the concrete, low-risk additions from §9 of that spec: canonical entry point, per-directory indexes, log.md "why" trail, link staleness checking, suggested-supersession detection, and reserved (inactive) frontmatter keys. It deliberately excludes anything depending on the unresolved federation/LSCT-Wiki open questions (§11, items 5–7) — those require operator decisions this cluster does not make.

## Goal

`smartdocs/` becomes a valid OKF v0.1 bundle (parseable frontmatter, non-empty `type` on every non-reserved concept, generated `index.md` at bundle root and each directory level, `log.md` on every doctrine/spec lifecycle transition) while every existing authority-tier, confidence-gating, conflict-detection, and agent-dispatch rule in `src/smartdocs-engine/` and `src/cognition/librarian-dispatch.ts` continues to behave exactly as today.

## Scope

Cluster-wide allowed paths (each child restates its own explicit scope):

* src/smartdocs-engine/**
* smartdocs/raw/**

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All children Done
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine` pass after each child
- [ ] No change to `librarian-dispatch.ts` rejection behavior (COGNITION_LOW_CONFIDENCE, COGNITION_SCOPE_VIOLATION, COGNITION_USER_SURFACE_PROTECTED, COGNITION_DOCTRINE_BLEED, COGNITION_SIZE_GUARD) — verified by existing test suite continuing to pass unmodified

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine

## Ordering

No dependency on other IMPLEMENT clusters. Internal child ordering below.

## Non-goals

* Do not build the LSCT-Wiki federation surface or a cross-repo link resolver (spec §10, §11 items 5–7 — unresolved operator decisions).
* Do not backfill federation metadata (`repo_id`, `authority_scope`, etc.) onto any existing document — reserve the keys only (spec §7.14, §9).
* Do not change the `raw → candidate → active → deprecated` directory-tier model or the doctrine one-way lifecycle.
* Do not modify `librarian-dispatch.ts`'s authority/rejection model.
* Do not change `.smartdocignore` eligibility rules or the migration tooling in `migrate.ts`.

POL-448.title: Add suggested-supersession detection to doctrine conflict checking
POL-448.body:
## Objective

Detect when an incoming doctrine-candidate document has high content overlap with an existing active document and surface it as a *suggested* `supersedes` relationship — never auto-applied.

## Context

Spec: `smartdocs/raw/smartdocs-2-0-architecture.md` §7.10. `supersedes`/`superseded_by` frontmatter fields already exist in the schema (`doctrine.ts` `ParsedFrontMatter`) but are populated manually today; there's no detection that a new doc probably should supersede an old one, unlike the existing conflict-detection path (`extractSpecKeywords`, `MODAL_REQUIRES`/`MODAL_PROHIBITS`, used by `specPromote`). OKF has no supersession concept to draw on here — this is a Polaris-only governance addition, consistent with the "never auto-decide, always ask when uncertain" rule already established for confidence-gated placement.

## Goal

Extend the existing keyword/scope-overlap extraction used by `specPromote`'s conflict detection to also compute a content-overlap score between an incoming `doctrine/candidate/` document and each `doctrine/active/` document. When overlap exceeds a threshold, add a suggested-supersession entry to the existing conflict report (`SpecConflict`-shaped, new `type: "suggested-supersession"`) rather than writing `supersedes`/`superseded_by` into any frontmatter. The user decides whether to accept the suggestion (by hand-editing frontmatter before promotion).

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/doctrine.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/doctrine.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New `suggested-supersession` signal added to the conflict report shape, alongside existing `content`/`map` conflict types
- [ ] Detection reuses the existing keyword-extraction functions rather than introducing a second algorithm
- [ ] Suggestion never writes to `supersedes`/`superseded_by` frontmatter automatically — report-only
- [ ] Unit tests cover: high-overlap candidate vs. active doc (suggested), low-overlap (not suggested), no active docs present (no-op)

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine/doctrine.test.ts

## Ordering

* None.

## Non-goals

* Do not auto-populate `supersedes`/`superseded_by` fields.
* Do not auto-deprecate the "superseded" active document.
* Do not change the promotion halt behavior for `content`/`map` conflict types — this adds a third, non-blocking, advisory signal only.

POL-447.title: Add two-tier link staleness checking (permissive raw/, strict candidate+active)
POL-447.body:
## Objective

Flag broken markdown cross-links in `smartdocs/doctrine/candidate/` and `smartdocs/doctrine/active/` (and the `specs/` equivalents) as a `stale-assumption` signal, while leaving `raw/` fully permissive per OKF's tolerate-broken-links rule.

## Context

Spec: `smartdocs/raw/smartdocs-2-0-architecture.md` §7.7. OKF §5.3 requires consumers to tolerate broken links everywhere — fine for the traversal layer, but a broken link from an *active* doctrine doc to a deleted concept is a real staleness signal Polaris's governance layer should not silently ignore. `docs-authority-model.md` §3.5 already defines a `stale-assumption` conflict signal for a different case (references to old API/structure); this child adds a second producer of that same signal for broken cross-links.

## Goal

A link-checking pass that: for every markdown link in a `candidate/` or `active/` doc pointing at another `smartdocs/**/*.md` file, verifies the target exists; missing targets are reported using the existing `stale-assumption` signal shape (see `detectDoctrineConflict` / `SpecConflict` in `doctrine.ts`) rather than a new one. Links from `raw/` documents are never checked (matches OKF's permissive default).

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/doctrine.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/canon-check.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/canon-check.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Link resolution implemented for `candidate/` and `active/` tiers only
- [ ] Broken links reported via the existing `stale-assumption` conflict signal shape — no new telemetry event type invented
- [ ] `raw/` documents are never link-checked
- [ ] Unit tests cover: valid link, broken link in `active/` (flagged), broken link in `raw/` (not flagged), bundle-relative (`/path.md`) and relative (`./path.md`) link forms both resolved correctly

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine/canon-check.test.ts

## Ordering

* None.

## Non-goals

* Do not build a full link graph or cross-link visualization.
* Do not auto-fix or remove broken links — flag only.
* Do not check links to files outside `smartdocs/` (e.g. links into `src/`) — out of scope for this signal.

POL-446.title: Add required log.md generation on doctrine and spec lifecycle transitions
POL-446.body:
## Objective

Record *why* a doctrine/spec document changed, not just *that* it changed, by appending a dated prose entry to a per-directory `log.md` on every `doctrineDraft` / `doctrinePromote` / `doctrineDeprecate` / `specPromote` call.

## Context

Spec: `smartdocs/raw/smartdocs-2-0-architecture.md` §7.9. Today `lifecycle.jsonl` and `audit.jsonl` record structured "that it happened" events but nothing human-readable. OKF's `log.md` (reserved filename, §7 of the OKF spec: date-grouped `YYYY-MM-DD` headings, newest-first, prose entries with a conventional bold-word prefix like `**Update**`/`**Creation**`/`**Deprecation**`) is exactly this missing piece. Per spec §11 open question 3, the "why" text source isn't fully resolved yet — this child adopts the default named in the spec (accept an optional `reason` string; fall back to an auto-generated summary line when not supplied) rather than blocking on full CLI wiring.

## Goal

Each of `doctrineDraft`, `doctrinePromote`, `doctrineDeprecate`, and `specPromote` gains an optional `reason?: string` field on their `options` parameter and, after their existing lifecycle-JSONL append, also appends a dated entry to `log.md` in the destination directory (creating the file with an `# Directory Update Log` heading if it doesn't exist yet). Entry format follows the OKF convention: `**<Draft|Promote|Deprecate>**: <reason or auto-generated summary>`.

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/doctrine.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/index.ts (thread an optional `--reason <text>` CLI flag through `doctrine draft/promote/deprecate` and `docs migrate`'s spec-promote command)
* src/smartdocs-engine/doctrine.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `log.md` created/appended at the destination directory for draft, promote, deprecate, and spec-promote operations
- [ ] Entries are date-grouped (`YYYY-MM-DD`, newest-first) per OKF §7 convention
- [ ] `--reason` CLI flag available on all four commands; omitted reason falls back to an auto-generated one-line summary (e.g. `"<file> promoted to doctrine/active/"`)
- [ ] Existing `lifecycle.jsonl`/`audit.jsonl` behavior is unchanged — `log.md` is additive
- [ ] Unit tests cover: fresh `log.md` creation, appending to an existing `log.md`, default reason fallback, explicit reason

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine/doctrine.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on the frontmatter-schema child (needs the schema doc's key documentation as the reference point for what "why" means) being Done first.

## Non-goals

* Do not remove or restructure `lifecycle.jsonl` or `audit.jsonl`.
* Do not make `--reason` required — OKF marks `log.md` optional at the spec level; Polaris chooses to always write one, but must not hard-fail a caller that omits `--reason`.
* Do not resolve open question 3 fully (e.g. auto-sourcing the reason from a Linear issue) — that's future work.

POL-445.title: Document SmartDocs frontmatter extension schema and reserve federation keys
POL-445.body:
## Objective

Write down SmartDocs's frontmatter extension schema (existing governance keys plus reserved-but-inactive federation keys) as a single documented source of truth, and extend the `ParsedFrontMatter` type to name the federation keys.

## Context

Spec: `smartdocs/raw/smartdocs-2-0-architecture.md` §7.5 (one schema doc for repo-local and federation-facing keys together) and §7.14 (federation metadata: `repo_id`, `project_id`, `authority_scope`, `inherits_from`, `upstream_standard`, `conformance`, `divergence_rationale`, `cross_repo_refs`). Per OKF §4.1, unknown frontmatter keys must already be tolerated and preserved — `ParsedFrontMatter` already has an index signature that does this at the type level. This child only names the reserved keys and documents them; it does not activate or populate them anywhere.

## Goal

1. A new spec doc, `smartdocs/raw/smartdocs-frontmatter-schema.md`, enumerating every current governance key (`kind`, `status`, `owner`, `source`, `confidence`, `recommended-action`, `overlap-analysis`, `implements`, `related`, `supersedes`, `superseded_by`, `depends_on`, `validates`, `source_paths`, ingest-provenance keys) plus the reserved federation keys from §7.14, each with purpose and default/inactive state, matching the spec's §7.14 table.
2. `ParsedFrontMatter` in `doctrine.ts` gets named optional properties for the federation keys (in addition to the existing index signature), purely for type documentation — no behavioral change.

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/doctrine.ts
* smartdocs/raw/smartdocs-frontmatter-schema.md (new)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Schema doc created in `smartdocs/raw/` with `status: raw` (per drop-zone rule — not directly promoted)
- [ ] `ParsedFrontMatter` interface documents the 8 federation keys from spec §7.14 as named optional string properties
- [ ] No existing frontmatter parsing/writing behavior changes (verified by existing doctrine.ts test suite passing unmodified)

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine/doctrine.test.ts

## Ordering

* None.

## Non-goals

* Do not populate `repo_id`, `authority_scope`, or any other federation key on any existing document.
* Do not build cross-repo link resolution or an LSCT-Wiki reader.
* Do not change `addCandidateGovernanceMetadata`'s default scaffold — federation keys default to absent/inactive, not to placeholder values.

POL-444.title: Generate bundle-root and per-directory index.md files for smartdocs/
POL-444.body:
## Objective

Generate a canonical bundle-root `smartdocs/index.md` and per-directory `index.md` files, giving SmartDocs the OKF v0.1 §6 progressive-disclosure entry point it currently lacks.

## Context

Spec: `smartdocs/raw/smartdocs-2-0-architecture.md` §7.2 (canonical entry point), §7.3 (breadcrumb traversal), §9. Currently each of ~45+ folders has its own `POLARIS.md` front door but there is no single repo-wide start file. `index.md` is an OKF-reserved filename with no frontmatter (except an optional `okf_version` key on the bundle-root file only).

## Goal

A generator, wired into the same write path `seed-instructions.ts` already uses, that produces:

1. One bundle-root `smartdocs/index.md` with `okf_version: "0.1"` frontmatter, sectioned by governance/routes, linking into `doctrine/active/`, `specs/active/`, and route POLARIS.md files (via the atlas).
2. A directory-level `index.md` for every `smartdocs/**` subdirectory lacking one, listing that directory's concept files with their frontmatter `description` (or title/filename fallback).
   Neither generator overwrites a human-edited `index.md` (reuse the existing `DRAFT_MARKER` skip-if-present pattern from `seedInstructions`/`seedSummary`).

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/index.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Bundle-root `smartdocs/index.md` generator implemented, reuses `DRAFT_MARKER` protection for human edits
- [ ] Per-directory `index.md` generator implemented, same protection
- [ ] Both wired into the same CLI/skill path as existing `seed-instructions`/`seed-summary` (no new dispatch mechanism)
- [ ] Generated files pass OKF §6 shape: no frontmatter on directory-level `index.md`; bundle-root `index.md` frontmatter contains only `okf_version`
- [ ] Unit tests cover: fresh generation, skip-if-human-edited, regeneration over an existing draft

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.test.ts

## Ordering

* None.

## Non-goals

* Do not replace or modify existing route-local `POLARIS.md` files — they remain authoritative.
* Do not generate `index.md` for `raw/` drop-zone directories (pre-classification content is not yet part of the addressable bundle).
